title: PRACTICE: Climate Change Solutions
description: In this exercise, you'll check your understanding of the different ways we can tackle climate change. You'll also evaluate which adaptation strategies can best help us cope with a warming planet. Let's see how well you understand these important climate actions!

choices.content: How we reduce the emissions of greenhouse gasses into the atmosphere to slow and limit climate change
How we respond to climate change to reduce its impacts on people, places, and ecosystems
The physical assets and systems that enable our daily lives, including transportation, energy, buildings, and sanitation
The idea that the challenges we're facing as our climate changes shouldn't affect any one community more than others
How we respond to climate change to reduce its impacts on people, places, and ecosystems
How we reduce the emissions of greenhouse gasses into the atmosphere to slow and limit climate change
An international framework that outlines climate goals
The process of people leaving their homes temporarily or permanently

assessment_items.question_content:
**What are the goals that climate change mitigation strategies try to achieve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Reducing, collecting, and storing greenhouse gasses
Building technical and social skills that help a community respond to a disaster
Ensuring food security and protecting vital infrastructure
Recycling plastics and reducing air pollution

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is mitigation important in addressing climate change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is the only way of limiting global warming
It is the only way to address the direct impacts of climate change
It is the only way to address the indirect impacts of climate change
It is the only way to keep a community functioning during a disaster

assessment_items.question_content:
**What’s the relationship between climate mitigation and adaptation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They are two solutions to climate change that may or may not overlap.
They are two solutions to climate change that are opposed.
Mitigation is a more effective solution than adaptation.
Adaptation is a more effective solution than mitigation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of a climate adaptation solution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Urban cooling centers
Low-carbon cement
Carbon capture and storage (CCS)
Nuclear energy

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of a climate change adaptation solution? Choose the best answer.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Weatherization
Electrification of the energy supply
Reducing greenhouse gasses in the atmosphere
Migration to cities

assessment_items.question_content:
**What are the four key areas of adaptation solutions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Agricultural systems, urban planning and infrastructure, ecological systems, and emerging technology.
Weatherization, planting trees around buildings, geothermal heating, and energy-friendly architecture
Coastal carbon capture, electric vehicles, nuclear energy, and green cement
Drought, heatwaves, intense storms, and food insecurity

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which of the following areas can we best make adaptations to respond to food insecurity issues?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Agricultural systems
Urban planning and infrastructure
Coastal carbon capture
Ecological solutions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following groups is particularly vulnerable to the impacts of climate change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Small-holder farmers
People living outside of cities
Communities near forests and wetlands
Societies with low poverty rates

assessment_items.question_content:
**How do economic factors make some communities more vulnerable to climate change impacts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People with limited resources are less able to prepare for, respond to, and recover from extreme weather events.
Extreme weather events affect everyone regardless of economic status.
Farming communities can better sustain themselves through drought.
Larger urban centers have more economic might to weather climate impacts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What problems do cities present in the face of climate change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They have vulnerable populations.
They are less susceptible to natural disasters.
They have lower carbon emissions.
They have natural defenses.
